name: SAM 2 (Segment Anything Model 2)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Meta research; no API costs; GPU required

auth.notes: Local inference; GPU required for practical speed

performance.notes: 50-200ms per frame on A100; real-time possible with smaller models on modern GPUs

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's universal image and video segmentation model. Segment anything in images/videos with point, box, or mask prompts. Zero-shot — no training needed. Multiple model sizes (tiny, small, base, large). Video object tracking across frames. Used for dataset annotation, medical imaging, robotic vision, creative tools. State-of-the-art zero-shot segmentation.

gotchas: GPU required for practical inference speed
Struggles with complex scenes with many similar objects
Video tracking can drift in long sequences
Large model variants require significant VRAM (24GB+)
best_when: Dataset annotation for object detection/segmentation training
avoid_when: When class-specific detection is needed (use DINO/YOLOv8)
Semantic segmentation with many categories (specialized models better)
summary: SAM 2 is Meta's zero-shot image and video segmentation model. Segment anything with point, box, or mask prompts — no training needed. Video object tracking across frames. Multiple model sizes for different GPU budgets. State-of-the-art zero-shot segmentation. Used for dataset annotation, medical imaging, and interactive creative tools.
